sections.paragraph:
ἄστοργος, ον (s. στέργω; Aeschin. et al.; Hellen. poets; Plut.; M. Ant. [Nägeli 17]; Athen. 14 p. 655c; IAndrosIsis 44; Kaibel 146, 6; 1028, 44; cp. adv. ἀστοργῶς IKyme 41, 21f) of one who is lacking in good feelings for others, thereby jeopardizing the maintenance of relationships (e.g. political and familial) that are essential to a well-ordered society; hardhearted, unfeeling, without regard for others in a catalogue of vices Ro 1:31; 2 Ti 3:3.—DELG s.v. στέργω. M-M.

Aeschin Aeschin , IV b.c.; spurious letters II a.d.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Hellen. Hellen. = Hellenistic. Not to be confused with the use of “Hellenic” as a national epithet.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

IAndrosIsis IAndrosIsis = Der Isishymnus von Andros und verwandte Texte—List 3

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

IKyme IKyme = Die Inschriften von Kyme—List 3

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 145
